key_highlights: Massive tech selloff led by chip stocks erased over $1 trillion in market value, with Nasdaq plunging 4.2% on fears of Fed rate hikes and AI trade exhaustion.
U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May, unemployment steady at 4.3%, but wage growth lagged inflation, complicating Fed policy.
Geopolitical risks remain elevated: Iran war nears 100 days, container rates spike 109% on Asia-to-US routes, and US sanctions Cuba's leader.
Healthcare sector shows positive catalysts: Dexcom CGM study shows benefits for non-insulin T2D patients, Novartis reports new Cosentyx data, and AbbVie gets EU approval for AQUIPTA.
Energy and commodity markets mixed: gas prices dip 17 cents, but corn, soybeans, and cotton slide; SAF production still disappointing.
M&A activity continues: Paramount may sell kids channels for EU approval of Warner deal, Salesforce acquires Contentful, Berkshire invests $10B in Alphabet's AI build-out.
Central bank divergence: ECB hikes rates, Fed signals caution, PBOC adds gold reserves.
Crypto under pressure: bitcoin tumbles to $60,000 amid jobs data and Zcash bug.

sectors.trend: Sharp selloff with Nasdaq down 4.2%, erasing over $1 trillion in market cap. Chip stocks lead decline as AI trade halts on Fed hike bets and concerns about AI boom sustainability.
sectors.implications: Investors rotating out of high-growth tech into defensives. AI infrastructure spending may slow if rate hikes persist. DoubleLine and Oaktree brace for potential AI pain. Berkshire's $10B investment in Alphabet's AI build-out signals long-term confidence but near-term volatility.

sectors.trend: Positive clinical data and regulatory approvals drive interest. Dexcom CGM study shows 82% of non-insulin T2D patients improve glucose levels. Novartis reports Cosentyx data in polymyalgia rheumatica. AbbVie gets EU approval for AQUIPTA. Amgen gets EU marketing authorization for IMDYLLTRA. Capricor Therapeutics and ImmunityBio gain attention as shorted small/mid-cap stocks.
sectors.implications: CGM adoption likely to expand beyond insulin users, benefiting Dexcom. Biotech M&A and pipeline catalysts remain key. Small-cap biotech with strong data may offer asymmetric upside.

sectors.trend: Container rates surge 109% on Asia-to-US routes since Iran war started. Delta Air Lines gets PT lift from Morgan Stanley, with strong premium travel demand. JetBlue CEO discusses potential consolidation at IATA. SAF production volumes still disappointing.
sectors.implications: Supply chain disruptions from Iran conflict boost shipping rates, benefiting container lines but pressuring importers. Airlines face higher jet fuel costs but premium travel demand supports Delta. SAF adoption remains slow.

sectors.trend: Gas prices dip 17 cents nationally. Corn, soybeans, and cotton slide lower. PBOC adds gold again. Raizen inks $13B out-of-court deal with creditors. US proposes new tariffs over forced labor.
sectors.implications: Lower gas prices provide consumer relief but commodity weakness signals demand concerns. Gold buying by PBOC supports bullion. Ethanol and agricultural markets under pressure from trade policy and weather.

sectors.trend: Scotiabank lifts PT on Royal Bank of Canada and Toronto-Dominion Bank. RBC Capital also lifts PT on TD. Fed's Barr warns of risks from looser Wall Street bank rules. BofA downgrades Nu Holdings on CFO transition.
sectors.implications: Canadian banks remain well-regarded with earnings support. US bank regulatory easing faces pushback. Fintech Nu faces leadership uncertainty.

sectors.trend: Paramount open to selling kids channels to quell EU fears over $110B Warner deal. Netflix names Jay Hoag as chairman. Broadway shows eye London, NY wooing with tax credits. Publishers adapt to AI.
sectors.implications: Media consolidation continues with regulatory hurdles. Netflix leadership transition smooth. Live entertainment recovery ongoing.

sectors.trend: Coca-Cola and Walmart top list of low-volatility dividend aristocrats. Bob's Discount Furniture shows improving financials. Domestic LPG price hiked by Rs 29 per cylinder in India.
sectors.implications: Defensive consumer staples attract flight-to-safety flows. Discount retailers benefit from value-seeking consumers. Indian inflation pressures from LPG hike.

key_themes: Tech selloff and AI trade exhaustion
Fed rate hike fears amid strong jobs data but sticky inflation
Geopolitical risk from Iran war and supply chain disruptions
Central bank divergence (ECB hawkish, Fed cautious, PBOC gold buying)
M&A activity across sectors (Paramount/Warner, Salesforce/Contentful, Berkshire/Alphabet)
Energy transition challenges (SAF, nuclear, data center power)
Labor market tightness with wage growth lagging inflation

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to high-multiple tech stocks, increase allocation to value and defensives. Consider hedging with put options on QQQ or SMH.
Shorten duration in bond portfolios, favor floating-rate debt. Reduce leverage. Hold cash for buying opportunities.
Increase exposure to energy, defense, and shipping stocks. Hedge via gold and commodities. Reduce exposure to regions directly affected.
Invest in logistics and shipping companies. Diversify sourcing. Monitor container rates and port congestion.
Favor TIPS, commodities, and real assets. Avoid long-duration bonds. Focus on companies with pricing power.
Reduce crypto exposure. Monitor regulatory developments. Bitcoin at $60k may attract dip-buyers but volatility remains high.
Avoid companies heavily exposed to forced labor allegations. Monitor US tariff proposals and Cuba sanctions.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy healthcare/biotech on weakness, especially CGM and rare disease plays
opportunities.rationale: Dexcom's landmark study expands TAM for CGM in non-insulin T2D. Novartis and AbbVie approvals provide catalysts. Small-cap biotech with strong data (Capricor, ImmunityBio) offer asymmetric upside.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate quality dividend aristocrats like Coca-Cola and Walmart
opportunities.rationale: Flight to safety amid tech selloff. Low-volatility, steady dividends provide downside protection. Consumer staples benefit from resilient spending.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in shipping and logistics companies benefiting from rate spikes
opportunities.rationale: Asia-to-US container rates up 109% since Iran war started. Supply chain disruptions support elevated freight rates. CONCOR and other logistics firms in focus.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider buying beaten-down tech selectively on the dip
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's $10B investment in Alphabet's AI build-out signals long-term value. Micron and Sandisk may be cheap for beginners. Tech selloff may be overdone if AI adoption continues.

opportunities.recommendation: Add gold and gold miners as geopolitical hedge
opportunities.rationale: PBOC continues buying gold. Iran war and US sanctions increase safe-haven demand. Gold under pressure but central bank buying provides floor.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce tech exposure and increase allocation to healthcare and consumer staples
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech selloff may deepen if Fed hikes. Healthcare has strong catalysts and defensive characteristics. Staples provide stability.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against rate hikes with short-duration bonds and floating-rate notes
defensive_moves.rationale: Strong jobs data and ECB hike increase odds of Fed tightening. Short-duration bonds reduce interest rate risk. Floating-rate notes benefit from rising rates.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash position to 15-20% for opportunistic buying
defensive_moves.rationale: Market volatility elevated. Cash provides optionality to buy dips in quality names. Avoid margin debt.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Buy put spreads on QQQ or SMH to hedge tech downside
defensive_moves.rationale: Nasdaq plunged 4% and chip selloff may continue. Options hedging can protect portfolios without full liquidation.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to companies with high exposure to Iran conflict or forced labor tariffs
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical risk elevated. US proposes new tariffs over forced labor. Iran war disrupts supply chains. Avoid companies with operations in affected regions.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. Tech selloff likely to continue as Fed rate hike fears persist and AI trade unwinds. Strong jobs data reduces recession risk but keeps inflation concerns alive. Geopolitical risks from Iran war and US sanctions add uncertainty. Defensive sectors (healthcare, staples, utilities) may outperform. Bitcoin and crypto remain under pressure. Expect further downside in growth stocks until Fed clarity emerges.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. AI investment cycle remains intact (Berkshire's $10B in Alphabet, Google's $1B/month SpaceX deal). Healthcare innovation (CGM, gene editing) provides growth. Central bank divergence may create opportunities. Container rate spikes are temporary but supply chain shifts may persist. US election cycle and geopolitical developments will be key. Long-term investors can accumulate quality names on dips.
key_catalysts: Fed FOMC meeting and rate decision (June 2026)
ECB rate hike impact on global yields
Iran war developments and potential ceasefire
US CPI and PCE inflation data for May/June
Q2 earnings season, especially tech and healthcare
AI regulation and government stake proposals (Trump)
World Cup 2026 preparations and economic impact
Crypto regulatory clarity and bitcoin ETF flows
watch_list: Nasdaq and SOX index technical levels (support at 15,000 and 3,500 respectively)
WTI crude oil (impact of Iran war on supply)
Gold price and central bank buying
Emerging market capital flows (Korea, India, Brazil)
